# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cloud monitoring and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
  - name: my-vm-instance
    type: compute.v1.instance
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
      disks:
        - deviceName: boot
          type: PERSISTENT
          boot: true
          autoDelete: true
          initializeParams:
            sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-10
      networkInterfaces:
        - network: global/networks/default
          accessConfigs:
            - name: External NAT
              type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT

  - name: my-cloud-sql-instance
    type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
    properties:
      region: us-central1
      databaseVersion: MYSQL_5_7
      settings:
        tier: db-f1-micro
        ipConfiguration:
          authorizedNetworks:
            - value: 0.0.0.0/0
          ipv4Enabled: true

  - name: my-cloud-storage-bucket
    type: storage.v1.bucket
    properties:
      name: my-app-storage-bucket
      location: US
      storageClass: STANDARD
      versioning:
        enabled: true

  - name: my-load-balancer
    type: compute.v1.globalForwardingRule
    properties:
      name: my-load-balancer
      loadBalancingScheme: EXTERNAL
      portRange: 80
      target: $(ref.my-target-proxy.selfLink)

  - name: my-target-proxy
    type: compute.v1.targetHttpProxy
    properties:
      name: my-target-proxy
      urlMap: $(ref.my-url-map.selfLink)

  - name: my-url-map
    type: compute.v1.urlMap
    properties:
      name: my-url-map
      defaultService: $(ref.my-backend-service.selfLink)

  - name: my-backend-service
    type: compute.v1.backendService
    properties:
      name: my-backend-service
      backends:
        - group: $(ref.my-instance-group.selfLink)
      healthChecks:
        - $(ref.my-health-check.selfLink)

  - name: my-instance-group
    type: compute.v1.instanceGroup
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      instances:
        - $(ref.my-vm-instance.selfLink)

  - name: my-health-check
    type: compute.v1.healthCheck
    properties:
      name: my-health-check
      type: HTTP
      requestPath: /
      checkIntervalSec: 10
      timeoutSec: 5
      healthyThreshold: 2
      unhealthyThreshold: 2

  - name: my-firewall-rule
    type: compute.v1.firewall
    properties:
      name: allow-http
      allowed:
        - IPProtocol: tcp
          ports:
            - '80'
      sourceRanges:
        - 0.0.0.0/0
      targetTags:
        - http-server

  - name: my-cloud-sql-user
    type: sqladmin.v1beta4.user
    properties:
      instance: $(ref.my-cloud-sql-instance.name)
      name: my-user
      password: my-secure-password

  - name: my-cloud-sql-database
    type: sqladmin.v1beta4.database
    properties:
      instance: $(ref.my-cloud-sql-instance.name)
      name: my-database

# Final secure version after review
resources:
  - name: my-vm-instance
    type: compute.v1.instance
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
      disks:
        - deviceName: boot
          type: PERSISTENT
          boot: true
          autoDelete: true
          initializeParams:
            sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-10
      networkInterfaces:
        - network: global/networks/default
          accessConfigs:
            - name: External NAT
              type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT

  - name: my-cloud-sql-instance
    type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
    properties:
      region: us-central1
      databaseVersion: MYSQL_5_7
      settings:
        tier: db-f1-micro
        ipConfiguration:
          authorizedNetworks:
            - value: <YOUR_TRUSTED_IP>/32
          ipv4Enabled: true

  - name: my-cloud-storage-bucket
    type: storage.v1.bucket
    properties:
      name: my-app-storage-bucket
      location: US
      storageClass: STANDARD
      versioning:
        enabled: true

  - name: my-load-balancer
    type: compute.v1.globalForwardingRule
    properties:
      name: my-load-balancer
      loadBalancingScheme: EXTERNAL
      portRange: 80
      target: $(ref.my-target-proxy.selfLink)

  - name: my-target-proxy
    type: compute.v1.targetHttpProxy
    properties:
      name: my-target-proxy
      urlMap: $(ref.my-url-map.selfLink)

  - name: my-url-map
    type: compute.v1.urlMap
    properties:
      name: my-url-map
      defaultService: $(ref.my-backend-service.selfLink)

  - name: my-backend-service
    type: compute.v1.backendService
    properties:
      name: my-backend-service
      backends:
        - group: $(ref.my-instance-group.selfLink)
      healthChecks:
        - $(ref.my-health-check.selfLink)

  - name: my-instance-group
    type: compute.v1.instanceGroup
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      instances:
        - $(ref.my-vm-instance.selfLink)

  - name: my-health-check
    type: compute.v1.healthCheck
    properties:
      name: my-health-check
      type: HTTP
      requestPath: /
      checkIntervalSec: 10
      timeoutSec: 5
      healthyThreshold: 2
      unhealthyThreshold: 2

  - name: my-firewall-rule
    type: compute.v1.firewall
    properties:
      name: allow-http
      allowed:
        - IPProtocol: tcp
          ports:
            - '80'
      sourceRanges:
        - <YOUR_TRUSTED_IP>/32
      targetTags:
        - http-server

  - name: my-cloud-sql-user
    type: sqladmin.v1beta4.user
    properties:
      instance: $(ref.my-cloud-sql-instance.name)
      name: my-user
      password: my-secure-password

  - name: my-cloud-sql-database
    type: sqladmin.v1beta4.database
    properties:
      instance: $(ref.my-cloud-sql-instance.name)
      name: my-database
